fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Carolina Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity south carolina, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to south carolina, speech-language pathologist compact south carolina, south carolina speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: South Carolina Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR)
board.mailing_address: SC Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation, P.O. Box 11329, Columbia, SC 29211-1329

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in South Carolina.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create an Online Services Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the SC LLR Online Services portal at https://eservice.llr.sc.gov/ to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your Online Services account, select the application for Speech-Language Pathologist licensure, and complete all required sections, paying the $120.00 application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official master's or doctoral degree transcripts from a regionally accredited institution to be sent directly to the SC Board of Examiners in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology. Electronic submission is preferred.

steps.title: Submit Clinical Experience Verification
steps.description: Ensure your supervisor completes and submits the "Verification of Supervised Clinical Experience (Form SLP-2)" directly to the Board office.

steps.title: Submit Praxis Examination Scores
steps.description: Request that ETS send your official Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology scores directly to the SC Board, using reporting code R7127.

steps.title: Verify Out-of-State Licensure (if applicable)
steps.description: If you hold or have held an SLP license in another state, arrange for that state's licensing board to complete and send the "Verification of Licensure in Other States (Form SLP-4)" directly to the SC Board.

telepractice_text: South Carolina permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: South Carolina issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: South Carolina is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a South Carolina SLP license cost?
Is South Carolina part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does South Carolina allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does South Carolina have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. South Carolina participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in South Carolina without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: South Carolina is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in South Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in South Carolina, you will submit your application and supporting documentation through the SC LLR's secure Online Services portal.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Reciprocity

official_links.registration_steps: Go to https://eservice.llr.sc.gov/, click "Register for an Account" on the left navigation, and follow the prompts to create your user account before logging in to start your application.
